course.title: Learn C with antirez
course.description: An interactive journey based on Salvatore Sanfilippo's lessons
course.subtitle: Bilingual educational platform (IT/EN)

author.title: Who is Salvatore Sanfilippo (antirez)?
author.bio_1: Salvatore Sanfilippo, known in the tech community as antirez, is one of the most globally influential Italian and worldwide programmers.
author.bio_2: He is internationally renowned for creating Redis, one of the most widely used open-source in-memory databases in the world (powering Twitter, GitHub, StackOverflow, and countless other companies).
author.bio_3: An absolute master of the C language, his code is universally studied for its incredible elegance, extreme performance, and pragmatism. Reading the Redis source code is considered a rite of passage and a formative experience for any aspiring C developer.
author.bio_4: This course was conceived and published by Salvatore completely for free for the community on YouTube. In these lessons, he conveys not just the C syntax, but the true expert mindset: deeply understanding what happens "under the hood", from source code, to CPU architecture, to Assembly, all the way to Linux kernel interactions.

developer.title: Developed by
developer.name: Claudio Dall'Ara
developer.bio: Passionate about systems, programming, and technology education.

asm_explorer.title: Assembly Explorer
asm_explorer.desc_o0: Without optimizations, the code is verbose. We see stack allocation and a call to 'puts' (replaced by the compiler for efficiency).
asm_explorer.desc_o2: With -O2, the compiler optimizes registers (xor eax, eax for return 0) and aligns the stack. It cannot use 'tail call optimization' (jmp) because main must clear the register before exiting.

home.hero_description: Interactive platform to master the C language through Salvatore Sanfilippo's lessons. From basics to Assembly, explained in a brutal and practical way.

features.editor_desc: Write C code directly in the browser, compilation happens via WebAssembly.
features.games_desc: Challenge yourself with interactive games designed to test your knowledge in a fun way.
features.simulations_desc: Visualize low-level concepts through high-fidelity animated simulations.

questions_info.title: Types of questions
questions_info.description: Basic questions (Quizzes) cover topics from the video, the transcript, or the lesson text. The "Pro Terminal Challenge" questions, on the other hand, are designed to push you further: they may cover material not explicitly taught and will require active research on external sources.

changelog.title: What's New in Version 1.0.2
changelog.page_persistence: Automatic saving of lesson reading progress.
changelog.video_persistence: Videos now automatically resume from the last watched second.
changelog.lesson_02_restoration: Restored original Lesson 2 depth and created technical Appendix.
changelog.simulator_improvements: Improvements to interactive simulators (6502 and Stack).
